message:
fix(orphans): reject --fix in non-interactive output modes

orphans --fix is interactive: it prompts the user to confirm closing
each orphaned issue before running br close. Combining --fix with
JSON, TOON, robot, or quiet output produced incoherent behaviour:
the prompts still ran (or were silently bypassed) while the surrounding
output mode said "this is machine-readable, do not write human text".

Add validate_fix_render_mode() which is called immediately after
resolve_render_mode(): if --fix is set and the resolved mode is not
Rich or Plain, return a Validation error explaining that --fix
requires human text output and listing the conflicting flags
(--json, --robot, --quiet, TOON via env/format).

Add a unit test that asserts the helper accepts Plain and Rich, and
rejects Json, Toon, and Quiet with a Validation error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
